name: Big Mama Thornton
description: American rhythm and blues singer and songwriter
abstract: Willie Mae "Big Mama" Thornton (December 11, 1926 – July 25, 1984), was an American singer and songwriter of blues and R&B. The Encyclopedia of Pop, Rock and Soul described Thornton by saying: "Her booming voice, sometimes 200-pound frame, and exuberant stage manner had audiences stomping their feet and shouting encouragement in R&B thea…

concerts.label: Boogie n' Blues at Carnegie Hall

about_quotes.speaker: Ingrid Sertso
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Have you ever heard Big Mama Thornton’s version? [INTERVIEWEE] No.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ingrid Sertso interviewed by Monk Rowe, Woodstock, New York, August 11, 2014

about_quotes.speaker: Ellis Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: I remember Big Mama Thornton came to the Dew Drop and she counted it off and I started playing a shuffle.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ellis Marsalis interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Orleans, Louisiana, January 7, 2020

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Who'd you work with on that? >> [INTERVIEWEE] Willie Mae Thornton...Count Basie...

about_quotes.speaker: Sammy Price
about_quotes.evidence: I had a really really strange experience with Big Mama Thornton down at the Cookery.

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy and Jeannie Cheatham
about_quotes.evidence: I had played for Big Mama all these years and she had never heard me sing.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy and Jeannie Cheatham interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 12, 1998
